project: Go CLI tool that turns any Linux machine into a ready-to-use dev environment via Docker + Tailscale
architecture: CLI (cobra) → internal packages (config, workspace, tailscale) → SSH to remote server → Docker containers per workspace, networked via Tailscale mesh VPN

conventions.naming: Go standard: lowercase packages, CamelCase exports, snake_case files
conventions.api: Cobra subcommands in cmd/devbox/main.go, business logic in internal/ packages via Manager interfaces
conventions.state: Interface-driven design: workspace.Manager and tailscale.Manager define contracts; implementations TBD
conventions.config: Per-project devbox.yaml parsed by internal/config; struct tags for yaml unmarshalling

recipes.new-command: 1. Add func {name}Cmd() *cobra.Command in cmd/devbox/main.go  2. Wire with rootCmd.AddCommand({name}Cmd())  3. Implement RunE handler calling internal/ packages
recipes.new-internal-package: 1. Create internal/{name}/{name}.go  2. Define Manager interface with methods  3. Implement concrete struct satisfying the interface  4. Import and use from cmd/devbox/main.go
recipes.add-config-field: 1. Add field to DevboxConfig struct in internal/config/config.go with yaml tag  2. Add validation in Load() if required  3. Update devbox.yaml.example

commands.install: go build -o devbox ./cmd/devbox/ && sudo mv devbox /usr/local/bin/
